segments.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
Tell Aaron and his sons to separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, which they make holy to me, and that they not profane my holy name. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “Tell them, ‘If anyone of all your offspring throughout your generations approaches the holy things which the children of Israel make holy to Yahweh, having his uncleanness on him, that soul shall be cut off from before me. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “‘Whoever of the offspring of Aaron is a leper or has a discharge shall not eat of the holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches anything that is unclean by the dead, or a man who has a seminal emission,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: or whoever touches any creeping thing by which he may be made unclean, or a man from whom he may become unclean, whatever uncleanness he has—
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the person that touches any such shall be unclean until the evening, and shall not eat of the holy things unless he bathes his body in water.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: When the sun is down, he shall be clean; and afterward he shall eat of the holy things, because it is his bread.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He shall not eat that which dies of itself or is torn by animals, defiling himself by it. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “‘They shall therefore follow my commandment, lest they bear sin for it and die in it, if they profane it. I am Yahweh who sanctifies them.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “‘No stranger shall eat of the holy thing: a foreigner living with the priests, or a hired servant, shall not eat of the holy thing.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if a priest buys a slave, purchased by his money, he shall eat of it; and those who are born in his house shall eat of his bread.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If a priest’s daughter is married to an outsider, she shall not eat of the heave offering of the holy things.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But if a priest’s daughter is a widow, or divorced, and has no child, and has returned to her father’s house as in her youth, she may eat of her father’s bread; but no stranger shall eat any of it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “‘If a man eats something holy unwittingly, then he shall add the fifth part of its value to it, and shall give the holy thing to the priest.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: The priests shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer to Yahweh,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: and so cause them to bear the iniquity that brings guilt when they eat their holy things; for I am Yahweh who sanctifies them.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron, and to his sons, and to all the children of Israel, and say to them, ‘Whoever is of the house of Israel, or of the foreigners in Israel, who offers his offering, whether it is any of their vows or any of their free will offerings, which they offer to Yahweh for a burnt offering:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: that you may be accepted, you shall offer a male without defect, of the bulls, of the sheep, or of the goats.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But you shall not offer whatever has a defect, for it shall not be acceptable for you.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Whoever offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to Yahweh to accomplish a vow, or for a free will offering of the herd or of the flock, it shall be perfect to be accepted. It shall have no defect.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You shall not offer what is blind, is injured, is maimed, has a wart, is festering, or has a running sore to Yahweh, nor make an offering by fire of them on the altar to Yahweh.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Either a bull or a lamb that has any deformity or lacking in his parts, that you may offer for a free will offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You must not offer to Yahweh that which has its testicles bruised, crushed, broken, or cut. You must not do this in your land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You must not offer any of these as the bread of your God from the hand of a foreigner, because their corruption is in them. There is a defect in them. They shall not be accepted for you.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
When a bull, a sheep, or a goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother. From the eighth day on it shall be accepted for the offering of an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Whether it is a cow or ewe, you shall not kill it and its young both in one day.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “When you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to Yahweh, you shall sacrifice it so that you may be accepted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until the morning. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Therefore you shall keep my commandments, and do them. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You shall not profane my holy name, but I will be made holy among the children of Israel. I am Yahweh who makes you holy,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. I am Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Unknown
